mission.title: cert-manager: Support adding custom annotations to generated secret
mission.description: Support adding custom annotations to generated secret. Requested by 26+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
Is there support for adding custom annotations to generated secret for syncing using kubed or similar?

If not, is this feature in the pipeline?

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support adding custom annotations to generated secret
steps.description:
:

This change introduces the concept of SecretTemplate for Certificates. When a certificate is issued, a new secret is created to hold the certificate data. This secret is created by cert-manager. In order to use solutions like [kubed](https://appscode.com/products/kubed/v0.12.0/guides/config-syncer/intra-cluster/) to copy this secret to multiple namespaces, this created secret must be annotated. 

SecretTemplate is a property of CertificateSpec. Labels and Annotations defined there will be

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support adding custom annotations to generated secret" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
:

This change introduces the concept of SecretTemplate for Certificates. When a certificate is issued, a new secret is created to hold the certificate data. This secret is created by cert-manager.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
